   </ix:exclude>
   <ix:exclude>&#xa0;</ix:exclude>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;"><b><i><em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Domestic Warrants, <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Ladenburg Warrants, <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Foreign Warrants, <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Ladenburg Warrants and <em style="font: inherit;"> July 2020 </em>Warrants </i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; text-align: justify; margin: 0pt;">In the <em style="font: inherit;">third</em> quarter of <em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> the Company entered into a purchase agreement (the <em style="font: inherit;">&#x201c;2019</em> Purchase Agreement&#x201d;) for the sale of (i) <ix:nonFraction id="c82558980" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2019-09-30_SubsidiarySaleOfStockAxis-August2019CommonStockPurchaseAgreementMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:StockIssuedDuringPeriodSharesNewIssues" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">4,198,566</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock and (ii) <ix:nonFraction id="c82558981" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2019-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019DomesticWarrantsMember" unitRef="Share" name="nby:ClassOfWarrantOrRightIssuedDuringPeriod" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">4,198,566</ix:nonFraction> common stock purchase warrants exercisable for <ix:nonFraction id="c82558982" contextRef="i_2019-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019DomesticWarrantsMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">4,198,566</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock (the <em style="font: inherit;">&#x201c;2019</em> Domestic Warrants&#x201d;) for gross proceeds of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82558984" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2019-09-30_SubsidiarySaleOfStockAxis-August2019CommonStockPurchaseAgreementMember" unitRef="USD" name="nby:ProceedsFromIssuanceOfStockAndWarrants" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">4.2</ix:nonFraction> million. The <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Domestic Warrants were issued with an exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82558986" contextRef="i_2019-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019DomesticWarrantsMember" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">1.15</ix:nonFraction> and an expiration date of <em style="font: inherit;"> February 13, 2025.</em></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">The Company allocated the proceeds between the common stock and <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Domestic Warrants by applying the relative fair value allocation methodology. The Company <em style="font: inherit;">first</em> allocated $<ix:nonFraction id="c82558989" contextRef="i_2019-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019DomesticWarrantsMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstanding" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">3.1</ix:nonFraction> million to the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Domestic Warrants, with the residual amount allocated to the common stock. See Note <em style="font: inherit;">13,</em> &#x201c;Warrant Liability&#x201d; for further discussion of the key assumptions used to value the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Domestic Warrants.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Ladenburg served as the placement agent for the transaction in exchange for a commission representing <em style="font: inherit;">six</em> percent (<ix:nonFraction id="c82558994" contextRef="i_2019-09-30_CounterpartyNameAxis-LadenburgThalmannAndCoIncMember" unitRef="Pure" name="nby:PrivatePlacementCommissionPercentage" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2">6</ix:nonFraction>%) of the gross proceeds, totaling $<ix:nonFraction id="c82558995" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2019-09-30_CounterpartyNameAxis-LadenburgThalmannAndCoIncMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:PaymentsOfStockIssuanceCosts" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.3</ix:nonFraction> million, and <ix:nonFraction id="c82558996" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2019-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019LadenburgWarrantsMember" unitRef="Share" name="nby:ClassOfWarrantOrRightIssuedDuringPeriod" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">167,942</ix:nonFraction> common stock purchase warrants exercisable for <ix:nonFraction id="c82558997" contextRef="i_2019-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019LadenburgWarrantsMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">167,942</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock with an exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82558998" contextRef="i_2019-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019LadenburgWarrantsMember" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">1.25</ix:nonFraction> per share and an expiration date of <em style="font: inherit;"> August 8, 2024 (</em>the <em style="font: inherit;">&#x201c;2019</em> Ladenburg Warrants&#x201d;). In addition, the Company reimbursed the Placement Agent $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559000" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2019-09-30_CounterpartyNameAxis-LadenburgThalmannAndCoIncMember" unitRef="USD" name="nby:PaymentsOfStockIssuanceCostsReimbursementOfExpenses" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-4">60</ix:nonFraction> thousand for certain expenses. The Company also incurred and paid other offering costs of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559001" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2019-09-30_CounterpartyNameAxis-LadenburgThalmannAndCoIncMember" unitRef="USD" name="nby:PaymentsOfStockIssuanceCostsOther" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.3</ix:nonFraction> million.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">The Company incurred total issuance costs of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559002" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2019-09-30_SubsidiarySaleOfStockAxis-August2019CommonStockPurchaseAgreementMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:PaymentsOfStockIssuanceCosts" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.5</ix:nonFraction> million in conjunction with the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Purchase Agreement. The Company allocated $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559004" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2019-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019DomesticWarrantsMember" unitRef="USD" name="nby:PaymentsOfStockIssuanceCostsAllocatedToWarrants" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.2</ix:nonFraction> million of the issuance costs to the warrant liability which was expensed in the Company&#x2019;s consolidated statements of operations and comprehensive loss during the period. The remaining $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559005" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2019-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019DomesticWarrantsMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AdjustmentsToAdditionalPaidInCapitalStockIssuedIssuanceCosts" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.3</ix:nonFraction> million was recorded as a reduction of additional paid-in capital in the Company&#x2019;s consolidated balance sheets. As the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Ladenburg Warrants were accounted for as a stock issuance cost, $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559007" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2019-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019LadenburgWarrantsMember" unitRef="USD" name="nby:PaymentsOfStockIssuanceCostsAllocatedToWarrants" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">59</ix:nonFraction> thousand was allocated to the warrant liability and expensed during the period and $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559008" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2019-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019LadenburgWarrantsMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AdjustmentsToAdditionalPaidInCapitalStockIssuedIssuanceCosts" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">65</ix:nonFraction> thousand was recorded as a reduction to additional paid-in capital in the Company&#x2019;s consolidated balance sheets. See Note <em style="font: inherit;">12,</em> &#x201c;Warrant Liability&#x201d; for a discussion of the key assumptions used to value the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Ladenburg Warrants.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">During the <em style="font: inherit;">third</em> quarter of <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> the Company and the holders of the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Domestic Warrants and the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Foreign Warrants entered into exercise agreements which resulted in the cash exercise of the warrants at a reduced exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559015" contextRef="i_2020-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019ForeignWarrantsMember" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">0.99</ix:nonFraction>. The Company received aggregate gross proceeds of approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559016" contextRef="d_2020-07-01_2020-09-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromWarrantExercises" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">6.8</ix:nonFraction> million from the exercises. The Company incurred and paid other offering costs of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559017" contextRef="d_2020-07-01_2020-09-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:PaymentsOfStockIssuanceCosts" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.2</ix:nonFraction> million. The Company also incurred and paid a $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559018" contextRef="d_2020-07-01_2020-09-30_CounterpartyNameAxis-ChinaKingtonMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:PaymentsOfStockIssuanceCosts" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.2</ix:nonFraction> million fee to China Kington for brokering the transaction, which equaled <em style="font: inherit;">six</em> percent (<ix:nonFraction id="c82559020" contextRef="i_2020-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019ForeignWarrantsMember" unitRef="Pure" name="nby:ExerciseOfWarrantsCommissionPercent" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2">6</ix:nonFraction>%) of the gross proceeds from the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Foreign Warrants.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">During the <em style="font: inherit;">third</em> quarter of <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> the Company and all holders of the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Domestic Warrants and <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Foreign Warrants entered into warrant repricing letter agreements. Pursuant to the agreement, in consideration for the exercise in full of the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Domestic Warrants and <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Foreign Warrants, the Company agreed to: (<em style="font: inherit;">1</em>) reduce the exercise price of the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Domestic Warrants and the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Foreign Warrants to $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559031" contextRef="i_2020-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019ForeignWarrantsMember" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">0.99</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;per share prior to exercise, and (<em style="font: inherit;">2</em>) in a private placement, issue new common stock purchase warrants (the <em style="font: inherit;"> &#x201c;July 2020 </em>Warrants&#x201d;) to purchase up to a number of shares of common stock, equal to <em style="font: inherit;">100%</em> of the number of <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Domestic Warrants and <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Foreign Warrants currently held by such holders upon the holders exercising their warrants.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">The <em style="font: inherit;"> July 2020 </em>Warrants became exercisable <em style="font: inherit;">nine</em> months after their issuance, for an aggregate of <ix:nonFraction id="c82559037" contextRef="i_2020-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-NewWarrantsMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">6,898,566</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock. The <em style="font: inherit;"> July 2020 </em>Warrants have an exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559038" contextRef="i_2020-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-NewWarrantsMember" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">1.65</ix:nonFraction> per share and will expire <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559039">five</span> and a half years after their issuance.&#xa0;The Company determined that the common stock issued from the exercise of the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Domestic and <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Foreign Warrants, and the <em style="font: inherit;"> July 2020 </em>Warrants to be <em style="font: inherit;">one</em> unit of account, and therefore did <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> allocate the proceeds between the common stock and the <em style="font: inherit;"> July 2020 </em>Warrants as, the proceeds, even if allocated, would be both recognized in additional paid-in capital.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">During the <em style="font: inherit;">third</em> quarter of <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> the Company also entered into a reprice agreement with Ladenburg which reduced the exercise price to $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559046" contextRef="i_2020-09-30_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-The2019LadenburgWarrantsMember" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">0.99</ix:nonFraction> per share and amended certain terms of the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Ladenburg Warrants. The Company&#x2019;s potential obligation to cash-settle the warrants if a specified fundamental transaction occurred was amended to apply only in situations within the Company&#x2019;s control. As further described in Note <em style="font: inherit;">13</em> &#x201c;Warrant Liability&#x201d;, the <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> Ladenburg Warrants were <em style="font: inherit;">no</em> longer classified as a liability as a result of this amendment.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b><i>TLF Bio Innovation <em style="font: inherit;">2021</em> Warrants</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">On <em style="font: inherit;"> January 15, 2021, </em>TLF Bio Innovation was granted warrants exercisable for <ix:nonFraction id="c82559052" contextRef="i_2021-01-15_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-TlfBioInnovation2021WarrantsMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightNumberOfSecuritiesCalledByWarrantsOrRights" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">15,000</ix:nonFraction> shares of the Company&#x2019;s common stock with an exercise price of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559053" contextRef="i_2021-01-15_ClassOfWarrantOrRightAxis-TlfBioInnovation2021WarrantsMember" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">0.6718</ix:nonFraction> (the &#x201c;TLF Warrants&#x201d;). The TLF Warrants will expire <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559054">five</span> years after their issuance. The TLF Warrants are classified as equity.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <ix:exclude>

   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b>NOTE <em style="font: inherit;">15.</em> EQUITY-BASED COMPENSATION</b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b><i>Equity Compensation Plans</i></b>&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">In <em style="font: inherit;"> October 2007, </em>the Company adopted the <em style="font: inherit;">2007</em> Omnibus Incentive Plan (the <em style="font: inherit;">&#x201c;2007</em> Plan&#x201d;) to provide for the granting of equity awards, such as stock options, unrestricted and restricted common stock, stock units, dividend equivalent rights, and stock appreciation rights to employees, directors and outside consultants, as determined by the Board.&#xa0; The <em style="font: inherit;">2007</em> Plan expired on <em style="font: inherit;"> March 15, 2017. </em>Upon expiration, new awards cannot be issued pursuant to the <em style="font: inherit;">2007</em> Plan, but outstanding awards continue to be governed by its terms. Stock options granted under the <em style="font: inherit;">2007</em> Plan expire <em style="font: inherit;">no</em> later than <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559076">ten</span> years from the date of grant. All stock options outstanding under the <em style="font: inherit;">2007</em> Plan were fully vested as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2020.</em></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">In <em style="font: inherit;"> March 2017, </em>the Company adopted the <em style="font: inherit;">2017</em> Omnibus Incentive Plan (the <em style="font: inherit;">&#x201c;2017</em> Plan&#x201d;), which was approved by stockholders on <em style="font: inherit;"> June 2, 2017, </em>to provide for the granting of equity awards, such as nonqualified stock options (&#x201c;NQSOs&#x201d;), incentive stock options (&#x201c;ISOs&#x201d;), restricted stock, performance shares, stock appreciation rights (&#x201c;SARs&#x201d;), RSUs and other share-based awards to employees, directors, and consultants, as determined by the Board.&#xa0; The <em style="font: inherit;">2017</em> Plan does <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> affect awards previously granted under the <em style="font: inherit;">2007</em> Plan. Upon adoption, the <em style="font: inherit;">2017</em> Plan allowed for awards of up to <ix:nonFraction id="c82559084" contextRef="i_2017-03-31_PlanNameAxis-The2017OmnibusIncentivePlanMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAuthorized" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">2,318,486</ix:nonFraction> shares of the Company&#x2019;s common stock, plus an automatic annual increase in the number of shares authorized for awards on the <em style="font: inherit;">first</em> day of each of the Company&#x2019;s fiscal years beginning <em style="font: inherit;"> January 1, 2018 </em>through <em style="font: inherit;"> January 1, 2027 </em>equal to (i) <ix:nonFraction id="c82559090" contextRef="i_2017-03-31_PlanNameAxis-The2017OmnibusIncentivePlanMember" unitRef="Pure" name="nby:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardAnnualIncreaseInSharesAuthorizedPercentageOfOutstandingCommonStock" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2">4</ix:nonFraction>% of the number of shares of common stock outstanding on the last day of the immediately preceding fiscal year or (ii) such lesser number of shares of common stock than provided for in Section <em style="font: inherit;">4</em>(a)(i) of the <em style="font: inherit;">2017</em> Plan as determined by the Board. On <em style="font: inherit;"> January 15, 2021, </em>the number of shares available for future awards under the <em style="font: inherit;">2017</em> Plan was increased by <ix:nonFraction id="c82561401" contextRef="d_2021-01-15_2021-01-15_PlanNameAxis-The2017OmnibusIncentivePlanMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfAdditionalSharesAuthorized" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">1,671,303</ix:nonFraction> shares. As of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em>there were <em style="font: inherit;"><ix:nonFraction id="c82561603" contextRef="i_2021-12-31_PlanNameAxis-The2017OmnibusIncentivePlanMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAvailableForGrant" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">1,842,993</ix:nonFraction></em> shares available for future awards under the <em style="font: inherit;">2017</em> Plan.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Under the terms of the <em style="font: inherit;">2017</em> Plan, the exercise price of NQSOs, ISOs and SARs <em style="font: inherit;"> may </em><em style="font: inherit;">not</em> be less than <ix:nonFraction id="c82559099" contextRef="d_2017-03-01_2017-03-31_PlanNameAxis-The2017OmnibusIncentivePlanMember_RangeAxis-MinimumMember" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:SharebasedCompensationArrangementBySharebasedPaymentAwardPurchasePriceOfCommonStockPercent" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0">100</ix:nonFraction>% of the fair market value of the common stock on the date of grant and, if ISOs are granted to an owner of more than <ix:nonFraction id="c82559100" contextRef="d_2017-03-01_2017-03-31_AwardTypeAxis-IncentiveStockOptionsISOMember_PlanNameAxis-The2017OmnibusIncentivePlanMember" unitRef="Pure" name="nby:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardPurchasePriceOfCommonStockPercentageOfStockOwnedByShareholderMinimum" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="1">10</ix:nonFraction>% of the Company&#x2019;s stock, then <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> less than <ix:nonFraction id="c82559102" contextRef="d_2017-03-01_2017-03-31_AwardTypeAxis-IncentiveStockOptionsISOMember_CounterpartyNameAxis-ShareholderOfMoreThan10PercentMember_PlanNameAxis-The2017OmnibusIncentivePlanMember_RangeAxis-MinimumMember" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:SharebasedCompensationArrangementBySharebasedPaymentAwardPurchasePriceOfCommonStockPercent" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="1">110</ix:nonFraction>% of the fair market value of the common stock on the date of grant. The term of awards will <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> be longer than <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559104">ten</span> years, or in the case of ISOs, <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> longer than <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559106">five</span> years with respect to holders of more than <em style="font: inherit;">10%</em> of the Company&#x2019;s stock. Stock options granted to employees generally vest over <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559108">four</span> years, while options granted to directors and consultants typically vest over a shorter period, subject to continued service. The Company issues new shares to satisfy option exercises under the <em style="font: inherit;">2007</em> Plan and the <em style="font: inherit;">2017</em> Plan.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

   </ix:nonNumeric>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">The aggregate intrinsic value in the table above is calculated as the difference between the exercise price of the underlying stock option awards and the closing market price of the Company&#x2019;s common stock as quoted on the NYSE American as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021&#xa0;</em>for options that have a quoted market price in excess of the exercise price. There were <ix:nonFraction id="c82559113" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:StockIssuedDuringPeriodSharesStockOptionsExercised" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="-3">no</ix:nonFraction> stock option awards exercised during the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021. </em>There were <ix:nonFraction id="c82559114" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:StockIssuedDuringPeriodSharesStockOptionsExercised" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-4">20</ix:nonFraction> thousand stock option awards exercised during the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2020 </em>for which the Company received cash payments of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559115" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromStockOptionsExercised" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">7</ix:nonFraction> thousand. There was <ix:nonFraction id="c82559116" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsExercisesInPeriodTotalIntrinsicValue" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="-3">no</ix:nonFraction> intrinsic value for stock option awards exercised for the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2020. </em>There were <ix:nonFraction id="c82559117" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:StockIssuedDuringPeriodSharesStockOptionsExercised" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">83</ix:nonFraction> thousand stock option awards exercised for the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2019 </em>for which the Company received cash payments of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559118" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromStockOptionsExercised" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.2</ix:nonFraction> million. There was <ix:nonFraction id="c82559119" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsExercisesInPeriodTotalIntrinsicValue" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="-3">no</ix:nonFraction> intrinsic value for stock option awards exercised for the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2019.</em></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">As of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em>total unrecognized compensation cost related to unvested stock options and restricted stock was approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559120" contextRef="i_2021-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:EmployeeServiceShareBasedCompensationNonvestedAwardsTotalCompensationCostNotYetRecognized" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">1.2</ix:nonFraction> million. This amount is expected to be recognized as stock-based compensation expense in the Company&#x2019;s consolidated statements of operations and comprehensive loss over the remaining weighted average vesting period of <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="us-gaap:EmployeeServiceShareBasedCompensationNonvestedAwardsTotalCompensationCostNotYetRecognizedPeriodForRecognition1" id="c82559121" format="ixt-sec:duryear">2.29</ix:nonNumeric> years.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b><i>Stock Option Awards to Employees and Directors</i></b>&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">The Company grants options to purchase common stock to its employees and directors at prices equal to or greater than the market value of the stock on the dates the options are granted. The Company has estimated the value of stock option awards as of the date of grant by applying the Black-Scholes option pricing model using the single-option valuation approach. The application of this valuation model involves assumptions that are judgmental and subjective in nature. See Note <em style="font: inherit;">2,</em> &#x201c;Summary of Significant Account Policies,&#x201d; for a description of the accounting policies that the Company applied to value its stock-based awards.&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">During the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em><em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> the Company granted options to employees and directors to purchase an aggregate of <ix:nonFraction id="c82559125" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_TitleOfIndividualAxis-EmployeesAndDirectorsMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsGrantsInPeriodGross" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">516,000</ix:nonFraction>, <ix:nonFraction id="c82559126" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31_TitleOfIndividualAxis-EmployeesAndDirectorsMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsGrantsInPeriodGross" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">1,158,000</ix:nonFraction>, and <ix:nonFraction id="c82559127" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_TitleOfIndividualAxis-EmployeesAndDirectorsMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsGrantsInPeriodGross" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">145,000</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock, respectively.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

   </ix:nonNumeric>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b><i>Expected Price Volatility</i></b>&#x2014;This is a measure of the amount by which the stock price has fluctuated or is expected to fluctuate. The computation of expected volatility was based on the historical volatility of our own stock.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b><i>Expected Term</i></b>&#x2014;This is the period of time over which the options granted are expected to remain outstanding. The expected life assumption is based on the Company&#x2019;s historical data.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b><i>Risk-Free Interest Rate</i></b>&#x2014;This is the U.S. Treasury rate for the week of the grant having a term approximating the expected life of the option.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b><i>Dividend Yield</i></b>&#x2014;We have <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> made any dividend payments nor do we have plans to pay dividends in the foreseeable future.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Forfeitures are estimated at the time of grant and reduce compensation expense ratably over the vesting period. This estimate is adjusted periodically based on the extent to which actual forfeitures differ, or are expected to differ, from the previous estimate.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">During the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em>the Company granted <ix:nonFraction id="c82559130" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockUnitsRSUMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardEquityInstrumentsOtherThanOptionsGrantsInPeriod" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">1,200,000</ix:nonFraction> RSUs to employees and directors. During the year <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2020, </em>the Company granted <ix:nonFraction id="c82559131" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockUnitsRSUMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-EmployeesAndDirectorsMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardEquityInstrumentsOtherThanOptionsGrantsInPeriod" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">160,000</ix:nonFraction> RSU to <em style="font: inherit;">one</em> employee.&#xa0;<ix:nonFraction id="c82559133" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockUnitsRSUMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-EmployeesAndDirectorsMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardEquityInstrumentsOtherThanOptionsGrantsInPeriod" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="-3">No</ix:nonFraction> employees were granted RSUs during the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2019.</em></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">For the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em><em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> the Company recognized stock-based compensation expense of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559136" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-EmployeeStockOptionMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.7</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559137" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-EmployeeStockOptionMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.5</ix:nonFraction> million, and $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559138" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-EmployeeStockOptionMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.4</ix:nonFraction> million, respectively, for option awards to employees and directors.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">The Company modified stock options held by Mr. Yonghao (Carl) Ma in <em style="font: inherit;"> April 2019, </em>Mr. Yanbin (Lawrence) Liu in <em style="font: inherit;"> May 2019, </em>Mr. Mark Sieczkarek in <em style="font: inherit;"> July 2019, </em>Mr. Todd Zavodnick in <em style="font: inherit;"> September 2019, </em>Ms. Gail Maderis in <em style="font: inherit;"> April 2020 </em>and Mr. Xiaopei Wang in <em style="font: inherit;"> August 2020, </em>who each resigned as a director of the Company. The option exercise period for each of the former directors listed was extended from <em style="font: inherit;">three</em> months to <em style="font: inherit;">three</em> years, calculated from each director&#x2019;s date of resignation. Each director&#x2019;s stock option awards became fully vested at the date of his or her resignation. In connection with the stock option modification for each of Messrs. Ma, Liu, Sieczkarek, Zavodnick, Ms. Maderis and Mr. Wang, the Company recognized stock-based compensation expense of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559141" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-EmployeeStockOptionMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-MessrsMaMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">14</ix:nonFraction> thousand, $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559142" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-EmployeeStockOptionMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-MrLiuMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">7</ix:nonFraction> thousand, $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559143" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-EmployeeStockOptionMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-MrSieczkarekMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-4">60</ix:nonFraction> thousand, $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559144" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-EmployeeStockOptionMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-MrToddZavodnickMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">24</ix:nonFraction> thousand, $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559145" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-EmployeeStockOptionMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-MsGailMaderisMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">36</ix:nonFraction> thousand and $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559146" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-EmployeeStockOptionMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-MrXiaopeiWangMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">17</ix:nonFraction> thousand, respectively, which are included in the figure above.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b><i>Stock-Based Awards to Non-Employees</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">During the year <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em>the Company did <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559147">not</span> grant options to purchase shares of common stock to non-employees. During the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2020, </em>the Company granted options to purchase an aggregate of <ix:nonFraction id="c82559148" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31_GranteeStatusAxis-ShareBasedPaymentArrangementNonemployeeMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsGrantsInPeriodGross" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">400,000</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock to non-employees in exchange for advisory and consulting services. Out of the <em style="font: inherit;">400,000</em> shares, <ix:nonFraction id="c82559150" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31_GranteeStatusAxis-ShareBasedPaymentArrangementNonemployeeMember_RelatedPartyTransactionsByRelatedPartyAxis-PartnerAndSeniorVicePresidentOfChinaKingtonMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsGrantsInPeriodGross" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">300,000</ix:nonFraction> shares were granted to a related party, Eric&#xa0;Wu, Partner and Senior Vice President of China Kington and the brother of Bob Wu, who serves on the Company&#x2019;s Board of Directors. Please refer to Note <em style="font: inherit;">19,</em> &#x201c;Related Party Transactions&#x201d;, for more details related to the consulting service agreement. During the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2019, </em>the Company did <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559152">not</span> grant options to purchase shares of common stock to non-employees.</p>

   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Stock options granted to non-employees recorded at their fair value on the measurement date and recognized over the respective service or vesting period.    </ix:exclude>
   <ix:exclude>&#xa0;</ix:exclude>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">The Company did <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559154">not</span> grant restricted stock to non-employees during the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021.</em></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">In connection with Mr. Sieczkarek&#x2019;s resignation, the Company granted <em style="font: inherit;"><ix:nonFraction id="c82561604" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-MrMarkSieczkarekMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardEquityInstrumentsOtherThanOptionsGrantsInPeriod" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">168</ix:nonFraction></em> thousand shares of fully vested registered stock to Mr. Sieczkarek during the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2019. </em>The expense related to these shares was recorded in the Company&#x2019;s consolidated statements of operations and comprehensive loss. At the time of his resignation, the Company also entered into a <em style="font: inherit;">two</em>-year consulting agreement with Mr. Sieczkarek under which he is entitled to receive additional shares of fully vested registered stock in exchange for consulting services. According to the terms of the agreement, the stock units are to be issued in <em style="font: inherit;">two</em> tranches of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82561465" contextRef="d_2021-07-01_2021-07-31_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-MrMarkSieczkarekMember_VestingAxis-ShareBasedCompensationAwardTrancheOneMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.2</ix:nonFraction> million each for a total aggregate fair market value equal to $<ix:nonFraction id="c82561466" contextRef="d_2020-07-01_2021-07-31_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-MrMarkSieczkarekMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.4</ix:nonFraction> million. The number of shares issued for each tranche is calculated using the closing price on each respective grant date. In <em style="font: inherit;"> July 2020, </em>the Company issued <ix:nonFraction id="c82559160" contextRef="d_2020-07-01_2020-07-31_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-MrMarkSieczkarekMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardEquityInstrumentsOtherThanOptionsGrantsInPeriod" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">192,983</ix:nonFraction> shares to Mr. Sieczkarek to fulfill the <em style="font: inherit;">first</em> tranche. In <em style="font: inherit;"> July 2021, </em>the Company issued <ix:nonFraction id="c82559161" contextRef="d_2021-07-01_2021-07-31_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-MrMarkSieczkarekMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardEquityInstrumentsOtherThanOptionsGrantsInPeriod" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">328,359</ix:nonFraction> shares to Mr. Sieczkarek to fulfill the <em style="font: inherit;">second</em> tranche. The expense related to the shares issued under the consulting agreement is being recorded over the term of the Consulting Agreement.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">During the <em style="font: inherit;">fourth</em> quarter of the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2019, </em>the Company paid <em style="font: inherit;">two</em> consultants, Ms. Moon and Ms. Xiao, via a combination of <ix:nonFraction id="c82559164" contextRef="d_2019-10-01_2019-12-31_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-MsMoonAndMsXiaoMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:StockIssuedDuringPeriodSharesIssuedForServices" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">36</ix:nonFraction> thousand registered shares and cash for services rendered, based on the terms of their consulting agreements.&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">For the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em><em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> the Company recognized stock-based compensation expense of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559167" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_GranteeStatusAxis-ShareBasedPaymentArrangementNonemployeeMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-4">240</ix:nonFraction> thousand, $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559168" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31_GranteeStatusAxis-ShareBasedPaymentArrangementNonemployeeMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">64</ix:nonFraction> thousand, and $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559169" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_GranteeStatusAxis-ShareBasedPaymentArrangementNonemployeeMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">37</ix:nonFraction> thousand, respectively, related to non-employee options and restricted stock grants.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="nby:LicenseCollaborationAndDistributionAgreementsTextBlock" id="c2077177" escape="true">
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b>NOTE <em style="font: inherit;">16.</em> LICENSE, COLLABORATION AND DISTRIBUTION AGREEMENTS</b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Transactions under the Company&#x2019;s major distribution agreements are recognized upon transfer of control of product sold to its major distribution partners at the amount of consideration that the Company expects to be entitled to. The Company records contract liabilities for the amounts that are estimated to be subject to significant reversal, including allowances for services, discounts, rebate programs, and product returns.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; text-align: justify; margin: 0pt;">The following table presents changes in the Company's contract assets and liabilities for the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 (</em>in thousands):&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 3px double rgb(0, 0, 0);"><ix:nonFraction id="c82559340" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:RevenueFromContractWithCustomerIncludingAssessedTax" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">6,599</ix:nonFraction></td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; padding-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0pt;">&#xa0;</td></tr>

   </ix:nonNumeric>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; text-align: justify; margin: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b><i>Avenova Spray Pharmacy Distribution Agreements and Specialty Pharmacies </i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Avenova Spray is made available in local pharmacies and major pharmacy retail chains under nationwide distribution agreements with McKesson Corporation, Cardinal Health and AmerisourceBergen. We have also entered into direct agreements with preferred pharmacy networks as part of our Partner Pharmacy Program. During the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em><em style="font: inherit;">2020</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> the Company earned $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559288" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_CounterpartyNameAxis-McKessonCorporationMember_ProductOrServiceAxis-AvenovaProductMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ContractWithCustomerLiabilityRevenueRecognized" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.6</ix:nonFraction> million, $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559289" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31_CounterpartyNameAxis-McKessonCorporationMember_ProductOrServiceAxis-AvenovaProductMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ContractWithCustomerLiabilityRevenueRecognized" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">1.7</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559290" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_CounterpartyNameAxis-McKessonCorporationMember_ProductOrServiceAxis-AvenovaProductMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ContractWithCustomerLiabilityRevenueRecognized" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">4.6</ix:nonFraction> million,&#xa0;respectively, in sales revenue for its Avenova Spray product from these distribution and partner pharmacy agreements.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Under these product distribution arrangements, the Company had a contract liability balance of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559291" contextRef="i_2021-12-31_BalanceSheetLocationAxis-AccountsPayableAndAccruedLiabilitiesMember_CounterpartyNameAxis-McKessonCorporationMember_ProductOrServiceAxis-AvenovaProductMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ContractWithCustomerLiability" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.9</ix:nonFraction> million as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559292" contextRef="i_2020-12-31_BalanceSheetLocationAxis-AccountsPayableAndAccruedLiabilitiesMember_CounterpartyNameAxis-McKessonCorporationMember_ProductOrServiceAxis-AvenovaProductMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ContractWithCustomerLiability" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.7</ix:nonFraction> million as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2020. </em>The contract liability is included in accrued liabilities in the consolidated balance sheets. The Company also recorded a prepayment of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559293" contextRef="i_2021-12-31_BalanceSheetLocationAxis-PrepaidExpensesAndOtherCurrentAssetsMember_CounterpartyNameAxis-McKessonCorporationMember_ProductOrServiceAxis-AvenovaProductMember" unitRef="USD" name="nby:ContractWithCustomerRebateLiabilityCurrent" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">19</ix:nonFraction> thousand and $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559294" contextRef="i_2020-12-31_BalanceSheetLocationAxis-PrepaidExpensesAndOtherCurrentAssetsMember_CounterpartyNameAxis-McKessonCorporationMember_ProductOrServiceAxis-AvenovaProductMember" unitRef="USD" name="nby:ContractWithCustomerRebateLiabilityCurrent" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">144</ix:nonFraction> thousand for rebates related to these distribution agreements as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2020, </em>respectively, that is recorded in the prepaid expenses and other current assets in the consolidated balance sheets (see Note <em style="font: inherit;">5,</em> &#x201c;Prepaid Expenses and Other Current Assets&#x201d;).</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;"><b>Over-the-Counter Sales of Avenova Spray</b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Avenova Spray was launched online on <em style="font: inherit;"> June 1, 2019 </em>direct to U.S. customers. Avenova Spray is offered primarily for sale on Amazon.com, the Company&#x2019;s website (Avenova.com) and Walmart.com. Avenova Spray was launched at select CVS stores and online on CVS.com in <em style="font: inherit;"> February 2021. </em>These channels provide the Company with more stable pricing and provide customers with easy access to our product. During the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em><em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> the revenue generated from Avenova Spray in these channels was $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559298" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_ProductOrServiceAxis-AvenovaDirectMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:RevenueFromContractWithCustomerIncludingAssessedTax" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">5.1</ix:nonFraction> million, $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559299" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31_ProductOrServiceAxis-AvenovaDirectMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:RevenueFromContractWithCustomerIncludingAssessedTax" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">3.3</ix:nonFraction> million, and $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559300" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_ProductOrServiceAxis-AvenovaDirectMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:RevenueFromContractWithCustomerIncludingAssessedTax" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-6">1.0</ix:nonFraction> million, respectively.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Deferred income taxes reflect the net tax effects of (a) temporary differences between the carrying amounts of assets and liabilities for financial reporting purposes and the amounts used for income tax purposes, and (b) operating losses and tax credit carryforwards.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

   </ix:nonNumeric>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">ASC <em style="font: inherit;">740</em> requires that the tax benefit of net operating losses, temporary differences and credit carryforwards be recorded as an asset to the extent that management assesses that realization is "more likely than <em style="font: inherit;">not."</em> Realization of the future tax benefits is dependent on the Company's ability to generate sufficient taxable income within the carryforward period. Because of the Company's recent history of operating losses, management believes that recognition of the deferred tax assets arising from the above-mentioned future tax benefits is currently <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> likely to be realized and, accordingly, has provided a valuation allowance.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">On <em style="font: inherit;"> March 27, 2020, </em>the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act was enacted. The CARES Act changed net loss carryforward and back provisions and the business interest expenses limitation. The Company has evaluated the impact of the CARES Act and determined that <em style="font: inherit;">none</em> of the changes would result in a material cash benefit to the Company.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">The valuation allowance increased by $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559371" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ValuationAllowanceDeferredTaxAssetChangeInAmount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">2.8</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559372" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ValuationAllowanceDeferredTaxAssetChangeInAmount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">1.3</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559373" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ValuationAllowanceDeferredTaxAssetChangeInAmount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">2.2</ix:nonFraction> million during the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em><em style="font: inherit;">2020</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> respectively.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">The entire amount of the unrecognized tax benefits would <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> impact our effective tax rate if recognized. The balance of unrecognized tax benefits increased as a result of a comprehensive analysis to substantiate the company's research credits. Accrued interest and penalties related to unrecognized tax benefits are classified as income tax expense and were immaterial. We do <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> anticipate that total unrecognized tax benefits will significantly change in the next <em style="font: inherit;">12</em> months. The Company files income tax returns in the United States and in California. Other jurisdictions are <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> significant. The tax years <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559380">2004</span> - <em style="font: inherit;">2021</em> remain open in the federal jurisdiction and <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559382">2006</span> - <em style="font: inherit;">2021</em> for California. The Company is <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> currently under examination by income tax authorities in federal, state or other jurisdictions.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

   </ix:continuation>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">On <em style="font: inherit;"> November 17, 2020, </em>the Company entered into a consulting agreement with Eric Wu. Eric&#xa0;Wu is Partner and Senior Vice President of China Kington and the brother of Bob Wu, who serves on the Company&#x2019;s Board of Directors.&#xa0;Pursuant to the Agreement, Eric Wu acted as a consultant to the Company in support of product expansion efforts as well as in potential financings and other transaction opportunities. The term of the Agreement was for <em style="font: inherit;">twelve</em> months.&#xa0;As consideration for his services, the Company granted Eric Wu options exercisable for <ix:nonFraction id="c82559502" contextRef="d_2020-11-17_2020-11-17_GranteeStatusAxis-ShareBasedPaymentArrangementNonemployeeMember_RelatedPartyTransactionsByRelatedPartyAxis-PartnerAndSeniorVicePresidentOfChinaKingtonMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsGrantsInPeriodGross" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">300,000</ix:nonFraction> shares of the Company&#x2019;s common stock under the Company&#x2019;s <em style="font: inherit;">2017</em> Omnibus Incentive Plan with an exercise price equal to the Company&#x2019;s closing stock price on the date of the grant and vesting on the <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559504">one</span> year anniversary of the grant date. Stock-based compensation expense of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559505" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_GranteeStatusAxis-ShareBasedPaymentArrangementNonemployeeMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-EricWuMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">152</ix:nonFraction> thousand and $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559506" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31_GranteeStatusAxis-ShareBasedPaymentArrangementNonemployeeMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-EricWuMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">15</ix:nonFraction> thousand was recorded for the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> respectively, related to Eric Wu&#x2019;s options.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>
  <div>
    &#xa0;
  </div>
  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31_UnusualOrInfrequentItemAxis-Covid19MemberMember" name="us-gaap:UnusualOrInfrequentItemsDisclosureTextBlock" id="c2077192" escape="true">
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b>NOTE <em style="font: inherit;">20.</em> PAYCHECK PROTECTION PROGRAM</b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">On <em style="font: inherit;"> May 6, 2020, </em>the Company received loan proceeds in the amount of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559580" contextRef="d_2020-05-06_2020-05-06_DebtInstrumentAxis-PaycheckProtectionProgramCARESActMember_LineOfCreditFacilityAxis-WellsFargoBankMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromIssuanceOfLongTermDebt" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.9</ix:nonFraction> million from Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. (the &#x201c;PPP Loan&#x201d;) pursuant to the Paycheck Protection Program (&#x201c;PPP&#x201d;) under the Coronavirus Aid, Relief and Economic Security Act (the &#x201c;CARES Act&#x201d;), which was enacted on <em style="font: inherit;"> March 27, 2020. </em>The terms of the PPP Loan were subsequently revised in accordance with the provisions of the Paycheck Protection Flexibility Act of <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> or the PPP Flexibility Act, which was enacted on <em style="font: inherit;"> September 5, 2020. </em>The PPP loan provided for an interest rate of&#xa0;<em style="font: inherit;">1.00%</em> per year&#xa0;and maturity&#xa0;<em style="font: inherit;">two</em> years&#xa0;after the date of initial disbursement, with initial principal and interest payments coming due late in fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2021.</em>&#xa0;The PPP Loan could be prepaid by the Company at any time prior to the maturity with <em style="font: inherit;">no</em> prepayment penalties. Funds from the PPP Loan could only be used for payroll costs, costs used to continue group health care benefits, rent and utilities incurred during the <em style="font: inherit;">24</em>-week period after receiving the PPP Loan (collectively, &#x201c;Qualifying Expenses&#x201d;) in order for the PPP Loan to be forgiven in whole or in part. The Company used the entire PPP Loan amount for Qualifying Expenses.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Since the Company determined that there was reasonable assurance that it would meet the conditions for forgiveness of the full loan amount, the Company accounted for the forgivable PPP Loan as a government income grant that we earned through the Company&#x2019;s compliance with the loan forgiveness criteria. A deferred income liability was recognized upon receipt of the forgivable loan proceeds. The deferred income liability was recognized as other income as Qualifying Expenses were incurred. For the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2020, </em>$<ix:nonFraction id="c82559587" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31_UnusualOrInfrequentItemAxis-PaycheckProtectionProgramCARESActMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:OtherNonoperatingIncome" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.9</ix:nonFraction> million, was recognized as other income and recorded in the consolidated statements of operations and comprehensive loss. <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c82559588">No</span> amount was recognized for the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021.</em></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">The Company received notice, dated <em style="font: inherit;"> May 24, 2021, </em>from Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. confirming the full loan amount of $<ix:nonFraction id="c82559589" contextRef="d_2021-05-24_2021-05-24_DebtInstrumentAxis-PaycheckProtectionProgramCARESActMember_LineOfCreditFacilityAxis-WellsFargoBankMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:GainsLossesOnExtinguishmentOfDebt" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.9</ix:nonFraction> million was forgiven.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;"><ix:exclude>&#xa0;</ix:exclude></p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>

    &#xa0;

  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="us-gaap:SegmentReportingDisclosureTextBlock" id="c2077193" escape="true">
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;"><b>NOTE <em style="font: inherit;">21.</em> SEGMENT REPORTING</b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">The Company&#x2019;s chief operating decision maker (&#x201c;CODM&#x201d;), who is the Company&#x2019;s Chief Executive Officer, allocates resources and assesses performance based on financial information of the Company. The CODM reviews financial information presented for each reportable segment for purposes of making operating decisions and assessing financial performance.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Prior to the DERMAdoctor Acquisition in <em style="font: inherit;"> November 2021 (</em>see Note <em style="font: inherit;">3,</em> Business Combination), the Company was managed as a single segment focused on commercializing Avenova Spray in the United States. After the DERMAdoctor Acquisition, the Company began managing and aggregating its operational and financial information in accordance with <ix:nonFraction id="c82559594" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:NumberOfReportableSegments" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="INF">two</ix:nonFraction> reportable segments: (<em style="font: inherit;">1</em>) Optical &amp;Wound Care and (<em style="font: inherit;">2</em>) Skin Care. The Optical &amp; Wound Care segment consists of products historically sold by NovaBay prior to the DERMAdoctor Acquisition. The Skin Care segment consists of products acquired in the DERMAdoctor Acquisition and skincare products subsequently sold under the DERMAdoctor brand.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Select financial information for each segment is as follows:</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="us-gaap:ScheduleOfSegmentReportingInformationBySegmentTextBlock" id="FB76D98C-A7F4-4F65-8498-EA8AE6AE21A0" escape="true" continuedAt="F9F3F42D-2F17-46A3-B696-C19D1EC0C11B">

   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;"><b>NOTE <em style="font: inherit;">22.</em> SUBSEQUENT EVENTS</b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">On <em style="font: inherit;"> January 19, 2022, </em>the Company amended the lease for its corporate headquarters located in Emeryville, California to extend the term through <em style="font: inherit;"> July 31, 2027.</em></p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">On <em style="font: inherit;"> January 31, 2022, </em>the Company&#x2019;s stockholders approved an amendment to the Company&#x2019;s Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation, as amended, to increase the number of authorized shares of the Company&#x2019;s common stock from <ix:nonFraction id="c82559611" contextRef="i_2021-12-31" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:CommonStockSharesAuthorized" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">100,000,000</ix:nonFraction> to <ix:nonFraction id="c82559612" contextRef="i_2022-01-31_SubsequentEventTypeAxis-SubsequentEventMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:CommonStockSharesAuthorized" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">150,000,000</ix:nonFraction> (the &#x201c;Amendment&#x201d;). The Amendment became effective upon the Company&#x2019;s filing of the Amendment with the Secretary of State of Delaware on <em style="font: inherit;"> January 31, 2022.</em></p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">In <em style="font: inherit;"> February 2022, </em>the Russian Federation and Belarus commenced a military action with the country of Ukraine. As a result of this action, various nations, including the United States, have instituted economic sanctions against the Russian Federation and Belarus. Further, the impact of this action and related sanctions on the world economy are <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> determinable as of the date of these financial statements. Although the Company does <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> have significant customers, suppliers or operations within the affected geographies, the specific impact on the Company's financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows is also <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> determinable as of the date of these financial statements.</p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

  <div>
    &#xa0;
